messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, ImageLoaded, Signed, SignatureStatus, TargetFilename, TamperingType, EventType_reg. Identify defense evasion tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host — BYOVD Kernel Bypass.
Host: DC-22  Actor: powershell.exe
  vulnerable_driver_loaded: gmer.sys (GMER rootkit scanner / arbitrary kernel r/w)
  kernel_arbitrary_read_write=YES
  kernel_callbacks_zeroed: PspCreateThreadNotifyRoutine, PspCreateProcessNotifyRoutine, PspLoadImageNotifyRoutine, ObRegisterCallbacks
  edr_callback_count_removed=4
  lsass_dump_immediately_after=YES

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Enterprise security tools occasionally use signed kernel drivers for elevated access. However, GMER rootkit scanner (arbitrary kernel r/w) is a known vulnerable driver on Microsoft's recommended driver block list — no enterprise vendor ships it as a current product.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  Driver gmer.sys (arbitrary kernel r/w): well-known BYOVD target with arbitrary kernel r/w capability. Kernel callbacks zeroed: PspCreateThreadNotifyRoutine, PspCreateProcessNotifyRoutine, PspLoadImageNotifyRoutine, ObRegisterCallbacks. These callbacks are how EDRs receive process/thread/image creation notifications — zeroing them makes the EDR blind to all new process and DLL load activity. LSASS dumped immediately after callback removal: credential theft while EDR is blind. 
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host DC-22: kernel-level EDR telemetry completely disabled. Process creation, thread creation, and image load events are no longer delivered to EDR. Attacker has full kernel visibility; defenders are blind.
[CONCLUSION] BYOVD kernel callback removal confirmed.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1562.001 + T1014 (Rootkit via kernel callback manipulation). Full host reimaging required — kernel integrity cannot be trusted.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host — Approved Kernel Driver Load.
  driver=BEDaisy.sys  product=BattlEye Anti-Cheat
  signed=True  on_ms_driver_blocklist=NO
  purpose=anti-cheat kernel driver  callbacks_modified=NO

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  BattlEye anti-cheat driver — signed, not on blocklist, does not zero EDR callbacks.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  Signed by developer. Not on MS driver blocklist. No callback modification.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized anti-cheat kernel driver — signed, approved, no callback tampering.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized game anti-cheat driver. No action.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1562.001 — AUTHORIZED KERNEL DRIVER. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
